JobApplication.name: Sajjad Hossain Sabbir
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Sajjad Hossain Sabbir
+8801933522941, sajjad.hossain1872@gmail.com, Dhaka, Bangladesh

May 16, 2026

Hiring Team
JANTRA

Subject: Application for Junior Web Developer (Remote)

Dear Hiring Team,

I came across the Junior Web Developer opening at JANTRA and wanted to reach out. The role caught my attention right away not just because of the tech stack, but because of how you described what you're looking for. Skills, problem-solving, and a willingness to learn. That's something I can stand behind.

I've been working with React.js, Node.js, Express.js, and MongoDB for over a year now, building real full-stack applications not just tutorials. I recently completed an intensive web development course at Programming Hero, and during that time I built three projects that I'm genuinely proud of. A matchmaking platform with role-based access for users, premium members, and admins. A community lost and found platform with location-based filtering and secure authentication. And a crowdfunding app where users can create campaigns, set goals, and track contributions through a personal dashboard.

Each of these involved designing UI from scratch, integrating REST APIs, writing backend logic, and handling authentication essentially the same kind of work you've described in this role. I also use TypeScript and Tailwind CSS regularly, and I've recently started getting into Next.js and PostgreSQL to grow my backend skills further.

I won't pretend I know everything. NestJS is still something I'm learning. But I pick things up fast, I take feedback well, and I actually enjoy figuring out how things work under the hood. I think that matters a lot in a junior role.

Remote work suits how I operate. I'm self-organized, I communicate clearly, and I stay on track without needing to be checked on constantly.

If you'd like to see my work, I'm happy to share my GitHub or portfolio. I'd love the chance to talk and learn more about what the team is building.

Thanks for reading,
Sajjad Hossain Sabbir
+8801933522941 · sajjad.hossain1872@gmail.com

JobApplication.name: Rafid Siddique
JobApplication.coverLetter:
I am a Full-Stack MERN & Flutter Developer with hands-on experience building scalable web, mobile, and AI-driven systems across startups, freelancing, and competitive environments. My work spans frontend, backend, DevOps, and AI integration, with a focus on production-ready architectures.

From a framework perspective, I primarily work with:

Frontend: React.js, Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Three.js (for 3D/web graphics)
Mobile: Flutter, React Native
Backend: Node.js (Express.js / Nest.js), REST APIs, microservices architecture
Databases: PostgreSQL, MongoDB (with Prisma/Mongoose)
DevOps & Cloud: AWS, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, GitHub Actions

In terms of what I have built, I have developed a wide range of systems:

Full-stack applications: Production-level web and mobile apps with authentication, API integration, state management (Redux Toolkit, Zustand), and scalable backend systems.
AI-powered systems: Designed a multi-agent AI automation platform (25+ agents) integrating LLMs, CRM systems, marketing tools, and analytics dashboards using tools like n8n and API orchestration.
Speech AI pipelines: Built ASR and speaker diarization systems using Whisper, LoRA fine-tuning, Silero VAD, and ECAPA-TDNN embeddings for long-form Bangla audio processing.
Secure systems: Developed device-binding and anti-piracy backend architectures to enforce one-device–one-app usage without compromising OS-level security.
Game development: Created interactive 3D web-based games using Three.js and React Three Fiber with real-time rendering and physics-based logic.
Data & automation tools: Built market research AI systems that analyze trends, ads, and marketplace data to generate product insights using scoring models.

I also have strong experience working in startup environments, where I handled end-to-end development, from system design and UI implementation to deployment and scaling. Additionally, my background in freelancing and business development helped me understand client requirements, product-market fit, and delivery under deadlines.

Overall, my programming experience is centered around building real-world, scalable, and intelligent systems, combining full-stack engineering with AI and automation to solve practical problems.

JobApplication.name: Jubayer Ahmed

I’m Jubayer Ahmed, a Full-Stack Software Engineer focused on building scalable, high-performance web applications with modern technologies like React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL. I enjoy turning complex ideas into clean, reliable systems that are fast, secure, and built to scale.

Currently at GeekSSort, I’ve developed 40+ production-ready APIs using NestJS, Prisma, TypeORM, and PostgreSQL, working across complete product lifecycles — from backend architecture and API design to responsive frontend experiences with optimized SSR/SSG. I’ve contributed to real-world platforms including real estate solutions, restaurant management systems, and an enterprise-level barbershop management platform designed with domain-driven design (DDD), modular architecture, and scalable service orchestration.

My backend experience includes designing secure authentication and authorization systems with JWT and RBAC, implementing caching strategies, optimizing database queries, and building APIs capable of handling high-load environments. On the frontend, I focus on creating smooth, responsive, and maintainable user experiences using Next.js and Tailwind CSS with clean component architecture.

I also have hands-on DevOps experience with Docker, Kubernetes, AWS (EC2, S3), and CI/CD workflows using GitHub Actions to improve deployment efficiency, scalability, and system reliability.

Alongside development, I’m passionate about competitive programming and problem solving. I’ve solved 600+ problems on Codeforces, 180+ on LeetCode, and 40+ on CSES, while also participating in contests like the ICPC Dhaka Regional, Meta Hacker Cup, and DUET IUPC. These experiences strengthened my analytical thinking, problem-solving ability, and performance under pressure.

Overall, I combine strong engineering fundamentals, practical product-building experience, and a problem-solving mindset to build efficient, scalable, and maintainable software systems.

​Sincerely,
​Md Soriful Hasan

I developed advanced paraphrasing and grammar correction systems similar to leading writing platforms, handling both frontend and backend architecture using Next.js, Node.js, Express.js, and MongoDB. JobApplication.name: Fahim Abrar
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Team at JANTRA,

I'm Fahim Abrar, a self-taught web developer from Bangladesh, applying for the Remote Junior Web Developer position. Your job description caught my attention because it focuses on skills and shipping real work — which is exactly how I've spent the past year teaching myself to build production software.

Here's what I've shipped recently:

→ AgentHub — a full AI agent rental marketplace with React, authentication, ratings, demos, and a rental system (https://agent-hub-rqtx.vercel.app)

→ SnapCV — an AI-powered resume builder with cover letter generation, ATS scoring, and a job description analyzer (https://snap-cv-two.vercel.app)

→ Multiple other shipped projects including an AI chatbot, AI PDF summarizer, and business landing pages

JobApplication.name: Omar Jafarli
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Hiring Team
JANTRA
Dear JANTRA Hiring Team,
I am writing to express my strong interest in the Remote Junior Web Developer position at JANTRA. As a Full Stack Developer with hands-on professional experience across the exact stack your team uses — React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL — I am confident I can contribute meaningfully from day one.
Currently, I work remotely as a Software Engineer at OwlStrix Security (Dubai), where I take ownership of both frontend and backend responsibilities. I build responsive interfaces, develop RESTful APIs, and contribute to AI-powered features — all while maintaining a strong focus on code quality, performance, and collaborative delivery. This remote-first environment has sharpened my ability to communicate clearly, stay self-motivated, and deliver results without constant supervision.
Before that, at Baku Technology Center, I developed and maintained production-level e-commerce and business web applications using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. I was responsible for translating Figma designs into pixel-perfect, production-ready interfaces, building reusable component libraries, integrating REST APIs, and continuously optimizing user experience. Earlier, at Martech MMC, I worked as a Freelance Frontend Developer delivering scalable solutions including e-commerce platforms and custom web applications for real clients.
What draws me to JANTRA is your focus on skills over credentials — a philosophy I strongly align with. My experience is built entirely on real-world projects, not just coursework. I am comfortable working across the full stack, I write clean and maintainable code, and I genuinely enjoy solving problems and learning new things along the way. I believe I bring exactly the mindset your team is looking for.
Some of the live projects I have contributed to include owlstrix.com, avankart.az, zirekdasima.az, bonamed.az, and qutb.az. I would be happy to walk you through any of these during an interview and discuss the technical decisions behind them.
I am excited about the opportunity to grow with JANTRA and contribute to your team's success. Thank you for your time and consideration — I look forward to hearing from you.

Warm regards,
Omar Jafarli
Full Stack Developer

RE: Application for Junior Web Developer

Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the Junior Web Developer position at JANTRA. As a recent graduate who earned a Bachelor of Science in Information Technology With Honors, I deeply appreciate JANTRA's focus on actual skills, problem-solving abilities, and a clean coding mindset over formal degree requirements. I bring a highly capable, hands-on foundation in React, Next.js, Node.js, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL that aligns directly with your technical stack and development goals.

Operating as a freelance Full-Stack Developer since 2024, I have consistently built modern web applications and converted UI designs into responsive user interfaces. My professional experience encompasses building scalable full-stack solutions utilizing Next.js, React, Node.js, and JavaScript/TypeScript. To ensure optimal system functionality and reliable data integration, I strictly avoid relying on standard connectivity frameworks; instead, my engineering approach focuses heavily on developing advanced core logic, intricate data structures, and efficient data management architectures. This strict focus on underlying logic and database optimization with PostgreSQL allows me to write clean code, meticulously fix bugs, and deliver highly reliable software.

JobApplication.name: Nadia Sharmin
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear JANTRA Hiring Team,

I am writing to apply for the Junior Web Developer position at JANTRA. With a background in Computer Science Engineering, hands-on full stack development experience through an industrial attachment at BooleanForce, and over a year of live production web work, I am confident I can contribute meaningfully to your team from day one.

Relevant experience

During my industrial attachment as a Full Stack Developer at BooleanForce, I worked on real-world web application development — building features, integrating APIs, writing backend logic, and collaborating within a structured development team. This gave me practical exposure to the full development lifecycle beyond academic projects.

In parallel, I have been working since 2024 as an E-Commerce & Website Specialist for Dropluxe, a Dubai-based business, where I independently developed and maintained a production website — handling UI implementation, database-driven product systems, performance monitoring, and iterative updates for a live international audience.

Alignment with your tech stack

Your core stack — React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL — maps closely to my existing skill set and the direction I am actively developing in. My backend experience with Node.js and Laravel gives me a solid foundation in server-side architecture and RESTful API design, which translates directly to NestJS patterns. On the frontend, I am proficient in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and have experience building responsive interfaces and consuming REST APIs. I work regularly with SQL and MySQL, making PostgreSQL a natural step up. Git and GitHub are a standard part of my daily workflow.

What I bring to the role

Across my academic projects and professional work, I have built and shipped functional software independently — from an online shipping system in Django and Bootstrap, to a blockchain-secured medical records system, to a University Time Scheduling System built with a Genetic Algorithm. I approach problems with a clean coding mindset and take pride in writing code that is readable, maintainable, and purposeful.

I am eager to grow within a focused engineering team, take ownership of tasks, and continuously improve. JANTRA's emphasis on skills and problem-solving over credentials aligns exactly with how I approach my own development. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can contribute.

JobApplication.name: Nethan Gabriel B. Bagasbas

I don't have years of professional experience on paper, but I have something I think matters more for a team like yours: consistency and a proven ability to teach myself anything the job requires.

In 2023, I started The Odin Project — an open-source full-stack curriculum — and stuck with it for over two years, logging 1,000+ hours across 30+ projects while carrying a full academic load. That's where I built my foundations in React, Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL, REST APIs, and Git — not from a classroom, but from actually shipping things and debugging when they broke. That same mindset carried into my internship at Syntactics Inc., where I identified a recurring architectural problem in a large Next.js codebase and consolidated scattered initialization logic into a single shared hook — cutting roughly 95% of duplicated boilerplate across the product.

My two production-deployed projects — Chirper (Next.js, Express, PostgreSQL, Socket.IO, Docker) and Calibrate AI (Next.js, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Vercel AI SDK) — reflect how I approach code: typed, structured, and built to last. NestJS is new to me, but its patterns map closely to the Controller → Service → Repository architecture I already use, so I expect the ramp-up to be short.

I'm not looking for a job to put on my resume. I'm looking for a team where I can do real work and keep getting better.

JobApplication.name: Sagar Thapa Chhetri
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am a Full-Stack Developer with hands-on experience building scalable web applications, Web3 platforms, and AI-powered systems using modern technologies such as Next.js, React, Node.js, AWS Lambda, and EVM-based smart contracts. I am excited to apply for the opportunity to contribute my technical skills and product-focused mindset to your team.

At Beyond Labs, I worked on building an AI-powered DeFi Copilot integrating LLMs such as OpenAI and Grok-3 to enable prompt-based blockchain interactions. I also developed serverless microservices using AWS Lambda, implemented test-driven development workflows, and integrated smart contracts across Polygon and Base networks. This experience strengthened my ability to work across frontend, backend, cloud infrastructure, and Web3 ecosystems in fast-paced environments.

Previously, at Nepsky (Bootwall R&D), I developed full-stack systems including a college management platform and news portal using React, Node.js, Express.js, and MySQL. I worked on scalable API design, authentication systems, role-based access control, and production deployments while also building responsive frontend interfaces and integrating Web3 functionality into decentralized applications.

Beyond professional experience, I actively build personal projects focused on DeFi automation, scalable backend systems, and modern full-stack architectures. My projects reflect strong problem-solving skills, system design thinking, and a passion for learning emerging technologies.

I am particularly interested in opportunities where I can contribute to innovative products involving scalable systems, AI integrations, and decentralized technologies while continuing to grow as an engineer.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my experience and technical background can contribute to your team.

Sincerely,
Sagar Thapa Chhetri

JobApplication.name: Raymond R. Palomares
JobApplication.coverLetter:
I’m a strong fit for this role because my background already aligns closely with the stack and the kind of work your team is doing. I have hands-on experience building full-stack applications using React, TypeScript, Node.js, REST APIs, SQL databases, and modern development workflows with Git and GitHub. I’m also comfortable working across both frontend and backend tasks, so I can contribute beyond just UI implementation.

On the frontend side, I’ve worked on responsive interfaces and transforming requirements into functional user experiences using React-based technologies. On the backend side, I’ve built and integrated APIs, handled database operations, and worked with server-side application logic. While I may not claim to know every framework at an expert level yet, I can ramp up quickly because I already understand the underlying concepts behind modern web development.

I also think I match what you value culturally. Your description emphasizes problem-solving, clean coding, and willingness to learn instead of just credentials, and that’s exactly how I approach development. I don’t just try to make something work — I try to understand why it works, how to structure it properly, and how to make it maintainable.

In addition, I’ve worked on real projects where I had to debug issues, adapt to changing requirements, collaborate with others, and deliver working software under constraints. That experience taught me how to learn fast, communicate clearly, and stay productive even when I’m unfamiliar with part of the stack.

What makes me valuable is not just knowing tools individually but being able to connect them together into working systems and continuously improve as a developer.

Dear JANTRA Hiring Team,

I am writing to express my enthusiastic interest in the Remote Junior Web Developer position. I was particularly drawn to your emphasis—that you care about skills, clean code, and problem-solving ability rather than academic credentials. This philosophy perfectly aligns with how I approach development: by focusing relentlessly on building functional, maintainable software.

My foundational knowledge covers the entire stack required for this role. I have hands-on experience working within modern JavaScript frameworks to tackle responsibilities like converting complex UI designs into responsive interfaces and integrating reliable REST APIs.

Front-End: Practical understanding of React and Next.js, allowing me to build dynamic user experiences using clean HTML/CSS/JavaScript (and TypeScript).
Back-End & Logic: Experience developing server-side logic with Node.js and structured application design using concepts similar to NestJS.
Database: Working knowledge of relational databases like PostgreSQL, enabling me to write efficient queries and manage data persistence effectively.
Process: I am comfortable utilizing Git/GitHub for version control and dedicated to the rigorous process of bug fixing and system maintenance.
As a junior developer, my greatest strengths are my willingness to learn quickly, my methodical approach to debugging (the root cause finder), and my deep commitment to writing clean, readable code. I view development not just as coding, but as solving puzzles.

I am excited about the opportunity to contribute my skills to JANTRA’s projects and grow within a remote, skill-focused team environment. Thank you for your time, and I look forward to the possibility of discussing how my dedication can help build your next generation of web applications.

JobApplication.name: Nadim Mahmud Nion
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Manager,
I am writing to apply for the Junior Web Developer position at Jantra . I came across this opportunity online and was immediately interested in contributing to your remote team, especially given your focus on building impactful digital products in a flexible environment.

I have a strong foundation in modern web development, with hands-on experience in React, Next.js fundamentals, Tailwind CSS, and state management tools like Redux Toolkit. Alongside my frontend expertise, I bring solid backend experience with Node.js, NestJS, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB. I have developed several full-stack projects, including a Medical Lab Testing system, PensionCare Management System, and the Cotton Group Website, where I focused on responsive UI, API integration, and performance optimisation.

One of my key projects, CompParts Hub, is a full-featured parts manufacturing platform where I implemented user authentication (including social login), JWT-based authorisation, payment integration with Stripe, and role-based dashboards for users and admins. The project emphasizes performance, secure data handling, and clean UI design using Tailwind CSS and Daisy UI.

Another notable project, Medical Lab Testing, involved building a secure REST API with role-based access control using NestJS, PostgreSQL, and Prisma. JobApplication.name: Sanusi Emmanuel Femi
JobApplication.coverLetter:
18 May 2026
Hiring Manager
Jantra
Remote
Re: Junior Web Developer — Full-Time, Remote
Dear Hiring Manager,
I am writing to express my interest in the Junior Web Developer position at Jantra. With three years of hands-on experience building full stack web applications, I have developed a strong foundation in creating clean, modern, and performant web experiences — which aligns directly with what your team is looking for.
My recent project, AquaGuard, is a real-time IoT water quality monitoring dashboard built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. It tracks live pH levels, turbidity readings, and WHO safety threshold alerts across multiple borehole sites in rural communities. Building this taught me how to handle real-time data, design intuitive interfaces under technical constraints, and ship production-ready code independently. You can view this and other work on my GitHub at github.com/hodlsensei.
Across my three years of experience, I have worked across the full stack — building REST APIs, designing responsive UIs, managing databases, and deploying applications. I am comfortable working independently in a remote environment, communicating clearly, and taking ownership of tasks from brief to delivery. I am eager to bring this same discipline and attention to detail to the work at Jantra.
I am excited by Jantra's focus on building clean, modern web experiences and would welcome the opportunity to contribute to your team. I am available immediately and happy to discuss further at your convenience.
Yours sincerely,

Sanusi Emmanuel Femi
Full Stack Web Developer
github.com/hodlsensei  |  x.com/hodlpreacher

JobApplication.name: Nishan Paul
JobApplication.coverLetter:
I'm a full stack software engineer based in Dhaka with a BSc in CSE from CUET and an ongoing MSc in Cybersecurity at Dhaka University. My core stack is Next.js, NestJS, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Docker - and over the past few years I've moved from building hobby projects to shipping production systems that are live, hosted, and used.

Fullstack Developer - Vertical Innovations Ltd. (2024 - 2026)
Built a Next.js and FastAPI smart dispensing platform with real-time inventory telemetry and custom punch-card authentication for industrial hygiene use.

I independently built and maintain four production-hosted applications under my open-source brand KAI:

Barcody - barcody.kaiofficial.xyz
Full-stack barcode intelligence suite built with NestJS, PostgreSQL, Redis, Socket.io, and BullMQ. Features real-time sync and async background job processing.

CMatrix - cmatrix.kaiofficial.xyz
Agentic red team platform built with FastAPI, LangGraph, LangChain, and Qdrant. Runs multi- agent orchestration with RAG-based retrieval and human-in-the-loop approval gates.

Markify - markify.kaiofficial.xyz
Markdown-to-PDF converter with real-time preview, built with Next.js and Shadcn UI.

Flick - flick.kaiofficial.xyz
Serverless P2P file sharing app using WebRTC and PeerJS. No server storage, no accounts.

All four are self-hosted on a Linux VPS with Docker Compose, Nginx reverse proxy, and Certbot SSL - which means I understand deployment and infrastructure, not just application code.

JobApplication.name: JARIUS Miguel Ballesteros
JobApplication.coverLetter:
COVER LETTER
Hi, I am looking for a company that help me to grow, learn, and challenge my potential
skills and talents. I think you and I can make a good team. You stand out as my potential
employer for me because I think we can help each other to work toward our goals. I can
offer my talents in tech, programming, or computers as you stretch me out by what I
can offer. I will share my all to the company helping them with my skills and what I can
offer.
I like building web sites. It makes me happy to build and see my finished application. I
am not just building a simple websites, I make sure that my website is at enterprise level
without sacrificing its User Interface. I make sure that my website are fully-functional
and not just a simple website. I want to solve other people problems in building and
writing systems and programs. I started with a HTML, CSS, JavaScript when I am in
college. I am exploring other ways to build websites such as using JavaScript
Frameworks such as Vue, React, Svelte with an integration of backend Framworks such
as Node JS, NextJS, and Express JS. I store my datas to MySQL, PostgreSQL, Supabase,
and MSSQL. Along with various JavaScript frameworks, I use API’s in my web
applications. Stripe for Payment Integration and Eleven Labs for voice translation are
one of my most used API’s, it makes my applications more realistic, profitable, and a
solutions to a real-world problem.
One of my System that I code is the Apartment System that make efficient to the
Landlord by managing their tenant’s problem may it be financially or environmentally,
The Ecommerce Management System that manages orders, customers, products, with a
real-world payment API such as stripe, and many cool features such as dark-mode of the
UI and an interactive UX. When a system, I will make sure that it is efficient and will solve
problems, or lessen the problem for the maximum possibility. I think the user, may it
would solve their problem, would it make them more happy?, will this application will
lessen their stress?. Being a developer is not just programming and writing codes, It
comes from building the web app with a heart. You can build a million of websites but if
no one is using it. No one is happy with your work, you are working for no one and just
for yourself. Social media plays an important role in our time that is why, most of my
websites are made to interact with each other, I added features such as chatting,
favorites section, customization of the products so if the customer is picky, he can
request to remove the ingredients to the product, or even request a desired menu that
is not yet there.
From the simple weather app to an enterprise level ticketing system, I have that in my
portfolio. Practicing my skills in building, writing, debugging codes everyday teach me
to be patient because all thing that is worth it, is worth to wait. You cannot just build the
app for one day and you will be good at it for the rest of your life. It takes me 3 years of
consistent learning to build a useful full stack web applications. My community help me
by recommending what tech stacks is in- demand, and mostly used in the industry. I
believe that being a website developer is a great start for my long-term career. I want to
secure my future. I want to influence other people with my skills, talents, or whatever I
can offer in building applications. I want to be a role-model to the aspiring web
developer that if you want to, you will not give up, you can make a way and your dreams

JobApplication.name: Md. Rubaet Kabir Zishan
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear Hiring Team,
 
I saw the Junior Web Developer opening at JANTRA and wanted to apply directly. The stack you are using React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL is what I have been building with, and the remote format works well for me. The clearest example of my relevant work is Campus Hub, a full stack platform I built using Next.js and Node.js with a PostgreSQL backend. I wrote the database schema from scratch using raw SQL joins, subqueries, indexed lookups without an ORM, and built RESTful endpoints for scheduling, resource booking, and student communication. I also handled role based access control so different user types (students, faculty, admins) each interacted with only their permitted views. On the frontend, I worked with Next.js to build out the interface and connect it to the backend APIs. PetPal was a similar exercise a Node.js and Express.js REST API backed by MongoDB, structured with MVC, covering pet listings, appointment booking, and caretaker matching. Beyond projects, I spent eight months as a software engineering intern at Aurify AI, a remote SaaS company in Chicago. The platform ran on Node.js and React, and my work involved contributing across the stack debugging, tracing root causes, and coordinating fixes with engineers. I wrote Playwright and JavaScript end to end test suites, validated REST API contracts through Postman, and worked within Agile sprint cycles. It gave me a solid understanding of how a real web product is structured, maintained, and shipped. I write clean, readable code and am comfortable with TypeScript, Git, and GitHub. I have not shipped a feature in NestJS specifically, but I understand its module structure and dependency injection patterns well enough to work within an existing codebase and get up to speed quickly.
 
If the background looks like a reasonable fit, I would be glad to talk further.

Frontend (React & Next.js): In my last remote role at Rent Yard, Inc., I turned 30+ Figma designs into clean, responsive components using React, Next.js, and TypeScript. I am comfortable handling complex state and making sure everything looks pixel-perfect across devices.

NestJS Ready: Because I write clean TypeScript and understand professional backend structures, I am confident I can pick up your NestJS workflow and start contributing code almost immediately.

I care a lot about writing clean code, solving tricky logic bugs, and learning new things fast. I'd love to bring that energy to your remote team.

JobApplication.coverLetter:
Subject: Junior Web Developer Job Application at JANTRA
Dear Morsalin Islam Alvy,
I hope you are doing well. I saw your post for the Junior Web Developer role at JANTRA and wanted to reach out. I am a junior web developer specializing in JavaScript, React.js, Next.js, and Node.js. I am very excited about this role and I want to bring my energy, hard work, and dedication to your engineering team.
I learned web development by building real projects from scratch. Through constant practice, I have learned how to write clean and organized code for both the front-end and the back-end. I know how to convert UI designs into responsive layouts that look great on all screens, and I am comfortable setting up REST APIs to connect the frontend with the backend.
I have built two main web applications that show what I can do. The first one is LibraRoom, which is a library study room booking platform. In this project, I used Next.js and Node.js to create a smooth booking system. I wrote backend logic to make sure users can check real-time availability and book rooms without any overlapping time conflicts. The second project is Tiles Gallery, an aesthetic showcase platform where I built dynamic search and filtering systems. Users can search for specific designs by title, and I also added private dashboards using JWT and BetterAuth so users can securely manage their profiles.
I know your job description mentions PostgreSQL and NestJS. While my current projects use MongoDB and Express.js, I have a very strong understanding of database operations, CRUD functions, and backend logic. Because I love learning, I am confident I can pick up NestJS and PostgreSQL quickly to match your exact tech stack.
I may be a fresher in terms of office experience, but I know how to debug errors independently using Chrome DevTools, read documentation, and solve problems on my own. I am highly adaptable, happy to take feedback, and ready to work hard on your development tasks without needing anyone to hold my hand. I also enjoy teamwork, which I proved when I helped organize big tech events for over 1,000 people at my university.
My resume and live project links are attached to this application. I would love to have a quick chat with you to discuss how I can help JANTRA build reliable software. Please contact me at +8801834489785 on WhatsApp or via email to schedule a time.
Sincerely,
Md. Nahidul Islam Nahid

JobApplication.name: Nadim Mahmud Nion
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Nadim Mahmud Nion
Konabari, Gazipur
nadimmahmud.webdev@gmail.com
26/5/2026

Hiring Manager
Jantra
Dhaka


Dear Hiring Manager,
I am writing to apply for the Junior Web Developer position at Jantra. I came across this opportunity online and was immediately interested in contributing to your remote team, especially given your focus on building impactful digital products in a flexible environment.

I have a strong foundation in modern web development, with hands-on experience in React, Next.js fundamentals, Tailwind CSS, and state management tools like Redux Toolkit. Alongside my frontend expertise, I bring solid backend experience with Node.js, NestJS, PostgreSQL, and MongoDB. I have developed several full-stack projects, including a Medical Lab Testing system, PensionCare Management System, and the Cotton Group Website, where I focused on responsive UI, API integration, and performance optimisation.

One of my key projects, CompParts Hub, is a full-featured parts manufacturing platform where I implemented user authentication (including social login), JWT-based authorisation, payment integration with Stripe, and role-based dashboards for users and admins. The project emphasizes performance, secure data handling, and clean UI design using Tailwind CSS and Daisy UI.

Another notable project, Medical Lab Testing, involved building a secure REST API with role-based access control using NestJS, PostgreSQL, and Prisma. I implemented Google OAuth and Firebase authentication for seamless cross-platform login and developed a file upload system using Multer to manage sensitive medical reports efficiently. This project strengthened my ability to handle real-world challenges like authentication reliability and secure data management.
Although my professional experience is still developing, I am a fast learner with a strong problem-solving mindset. I quickly adapt to new technologies and can efficiently pick up tools like React Native or advanced Next.js concepts to meet project requirements. I am confident that my technical skills, dedication, and eagerness to grow make me a valuable addition to your team.

I have attached my CV and portfolio for your review. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can contribute to Jantra. 

Thank you for your time and consideration.
Sincerely,
Nadim Mahmud Nion

JobApplication.name: Tauhid Kadir Taaha
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Honestly, the stack you listed — React, Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL — is pretty much what I've been using in my own projects anyway. I've built a telemedicine platform on Next.js + Node.js with PostgreSQL, a full portfolio site with React and TypeScript, a blockchain-based disaster relief platform, even an AI movie recommendation system using PyTorch and React. These are things I built because the problems were interesting, not because they were assigned.
NestJS is something I haven't used heavily yet, but I've worked enough with Express and Node that I don't think it'll take long to get comfortable.
The thing I'd actually highlight is my internship at Pathao as a Data Science Enfgineer. I worked across two teams — Pathao Food and Pathao Maps — on systems that millions of people use every day. That's a different kind of experience. You learn really fast when broken code affects real users. It also taught me how proper team workflows actually feel — code reviews, cross-team collaboration, shipping under pressure, all of it.
I'm comfortable with Git, REST API integration, responsive UI work, and I actually enjoy debugging — not just fixing the symptom but understanding why it broke in the first place.
Remote works fine for me. I'm independent, I communicate clearly, and I don't need handholding to get things done.

JobApplication.name: Zahid hasan
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Subject: Application for Junior Web Developer - Zahid Hasan
Hi Hiring Team at JANTRA,
When I saw your job posting for the Junior Web Developer role, the line that instantly caught my attention was: "We don’t care about your degree — we care about your skills." As a developer who spends nights building production-ready applications and competing in global hackathons, this mindset resonates perfectly with me.
I am writing to express my strong interest in joining JANTRA. I have hands-on experience building scalable architectures using the exact tech stack you are looking for: React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, TypeScript, and PostgreSQL.
Recently, I developed and deployed MarketPulse AI, a full-stack application leveraging a NestJS backend and a highly scalable Next.js frontend. Through this and other complex projects, I have gained practical experience in:
API & Backend Architecture: Architecting robust RESTful APIs with NestJS and Node.js, and aggressively debugging server-side issues to ensure reliable data flow.
Frontend Development: Converting UI concepts into highly responsive web interfaces using React and Next.js.
Database Management: Designing schemas and managing data securely with PostgreSQL.
Problem Solving & Resilience: Implementing fallback mechanisms and clean code architectures to ensure system reliability even during heavy API loads.
I am highly comfortable with Git/GitHub workflows and thrive in remote, fast-paced environments. Beyond just writing code, I care deeply about clean architecture, solving real-world problems, and I am always eager to learn and adapt to new technologies.
I have attached my resume for your review and included links to my GitHub repository and live projects below. I would love the opportunity to bring my problem-solving skills and passion for modern web development to your team.
Thank you for your time and consideration.
Best regards,
Zahid Hasan

JobApplication.name: Mahamudul Hasan
JobApplication.coverLetter:
June 1, 2026

Hiring Team

JANTRA

Remote

Dear Hiring Team,

Building production systems for a government client and shipping scalable event platform features before most developers have their first real job, that is the kind of track record I bring to the Junior Web Developer role at JANTRA. My technical stack — React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, PostgreSQL, TypeScript, and REST APIs — maps directly onto your requirements, and I have used every one of these in real, live deployments.

My Property Management System, deployed live for the Khulna DC Office, required me to design a normalized PostgreSQL schema, build secure RESTful APIs with Laravel and Next.js, integrate SMS gateway and online payment processing, and manage multi-role access workflows — covering database management, API integration, bug fixing, and responsive web design in a single government-grade project. On the Event Platform, I used Node.js and NestJS to build modular backend features, integrated third-party APIs, and optimized database queries, reducing page load time by 45%. Both systems run in production today.

I write clean, typed TypeScript, collaborate naturally on a team, fix bugs methodically, and adapt to new technologies fast. I am ready to contribute from day one at JANTRA. I would welcome the chance to discuss how I can support your team — let's connect.

Sincerely,

Mahamudul Hasan Ratul

Junior Web Developer

ratulhasan048@gmail.com | 01717160829

JobApplication.name: Abdul Hamid Tanim
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Dear sir,
I am writing to express my strong interest in a Software Engineer position at your company . I 
recently completed my B.Sc in Computer Science and Engineering from the American 
International University of Bangladesh (AIUB) in January 2025, graduating with a CGPA of 3.79 
and earning Dean’s List Honours three times. I am eager to contribute my technical skills, 
collaborative mindset, and passion for software development to your team. 
I bring practical industry experience from my role as a Front-end Developer Intern at Unified 
Mentor (Remote, October 2024 – January 2025), where I built responsive web pages using HTML, 
CSS, and JavaScript, and improved website features to enhance usability and meet project 
requirements. This experience gave me hands-on exposure to real-world development workflows, 
collaborative teamwork, and delivering user-focused solutions under deadlines. 
On the backend, I am proficient in ASP.NET Core MVC, .NET, Entity Framework, MS SQL Server, 
and PostgreSQL, and I have applied these technologies in building production-ready applications. 
My most notable project is a full-stack E-Commerce Application that includes CRUD operations, 
user authentication, role-based authorization, and an admin dashboard with a responsive 
Bootstrap UI. I have also Dockerized an ASP.NET Core application with SQL Server, making it 
fully portable and environment-independent. Additionally, I collaborated on a group project — Car 
Fuel Service — using Git and an ASP.NET backend with an Angular frontend, which strengthened 
my ability to work in a team-based development environment. 
I have solved over 100 data structure and algorithmic problems on LeetCode, earning both the 50 
Days and 100 Days badges in 2025, which reflects my commitment to strong problem-solving 
fundamentals. I also hold Udemy certifications in .NET Core MVC, PostgreSQL, JavaScript, and 
Git, and I have conducted academic research on YOLO object detection models, demonstrating 
my ability to engage with complex technical literature. 
I am highly motivated to grow within a dynamic and collaborative team, and I am confident that 
my technical background, hands-on project experience, and continuous learning approach make 
me a strong candidate for this role. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can 
contribute for your company. Thank you sincerely for your time and consideration. 
Sincerely, 
Md. Abdul Hamid Tanim
B.Sc in CSE, AIUB mdabdulhamidtanim@gmail.com 
01747058503

JobApplication.name: Jarius Miguel Ballesteros
JobApplication.coverLetter:
COVER LETTER


Hi, I am looking for a company that help me to grow, learn, and challenge my potential skills and talents. I think you and I can make a good team. You stand out as my potential employer for me because I think we can help each other to work toward our goals. I can offer my talents in tech, programming, or computers as you stretch me out by what I can offer. I will share my all to the company helping them with my skills and what I can offer. 

I like building web sites. It makes me happy to build and see my finished application. I am not just building a simple websites, I make sure that my website is at enterprise level without sacrificing its User Interface. I make sure that my website are fully-functional and not just a simple website. I want to solve other people problems in building and writing systems and programs. I started with a HTML, CSS, JavaScript when I am in college. I am exploring other ways to build websites such as using JavaScript Frameworks such as Vue, React, Svelte with an integration of backend Framworks such as Node JS, NextJS, and Express JS. I store my datas to MySQL, PostgreSQL, Supabase, and MSSQL. Along with various  JavaScript frameworks, I use API’s in my web applications. Stripe for Payment Integration and Eleven Labs for voice translation are one of my most used API’s, it makes my applications more realistic, profitable, and a solutions to a real-world problem.

 One of my System that I code is the Apartment System that make efficient to the Landlord by managing their tenant’s problem may it be financially or environmentally, The Ecommerce Management System that manages orders, customers, products, with a real-world payment API such as stripe, and many cool features such as dark-mode of the UI and an interactive UX. When a system, I will make sure that it is efficient and will solve problems, or lessen the problem for the maximum possibility. I think the user, may it would solve their problem, would it make them more happy?, will this application will lessen their stress?. Being a developer is not just programming and writing codes, It comes from building the web app with a heart. You can build a million of websites but if no one is using it. No one is happy with your work, you are working for no one and just for yourself.  Social media plays an important role in our time that is why, most of my websites are made to interact with each other, I added features such as chatting, favorites section, customization of the products so if the customer is picky, he can request to remove the ingredients to the product, or even request a desired menu that is not yet there.

From the simple weather app to an enterprise level ticketing system, I have that in my portfolio. Practicing my skills in building, writing, debugging codes everyday  teach me to be patient because all thing that is worth it, is worth to wait. You cannot just build the app for one day and you will be good at it for the rest of your life. It takes me 3 years of consistent learning to build a useful full stack web applications. My community help me by recommending what tech stacks is in- demand, and mostly used in the industry. I believe that being a website developer is a great start for my long-term career. 

I would like to consider being a Network Engineer, IT Support, Helpdesk Support, DevOps Engineer, Data Analyst, or any job that creates technology.

I want to secure my future. I want to influence other people with my skills, talents, or whatever I can offer in building applications. I want to be a role-model to the aspiring web developer that if you want to, you will not give up, you can make a way and your dreams will come true.

Hi, 
I’m a Full Stack Developer with strong experience building and maintaining production-ready 
applications, with a focus on correctness, performance, and clean integration between frontend and 
backend systems. 
I work across technologies including React, Next.js, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, MySQL, and

data flow issues, and align UI behavior with backend logic. I especially enjoy cleanup and integration

maintainability. 
I prioritize: 
● Following backend data as the single source of truth 
● Minimal, focused changes that improve correctness 
● Clear communication and incremental delivery 
I’d be happy to review your existing setup and contribute where my skills add the most value. 
Thank you for your time and consideration.

JobApplication.name: Kabya Kishor Halder
JobApplication.coverLetter:
My most rewarding full-stack project is MediQueue, a responsive Tutor Booking Platform built using Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Node.js.

It was highly rewarding because I successfully solved the complete end-to-end user loop—connecting a pixel-perfect frontend seamlessly to secure RESTful APIs and a MongoDB database, resulting in a production-ready system deployed smoothly via Vercel.

JobApplication.name: Kazi Md. Osman Goni
JobApplication.coverLetter:
Kazi Md. Osman Goni
kaziosman873@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/kazimdosmangoni | github.com/osman87326 | 01871211687


Dear Hiring Team at JANTRA,

I'm writing to apply for the Junior Web Developer position at JANTRA. Having built and deployed production-grade full-stack applications using Next.js, Node.js, and MongoDB, I'm confident I can contribute meaningfully to your team from day one — not as someone still finding their footing, but as a developer who has already solved real engineering problems.

My core stack aligns directly with what you're looking for: Next.js, React, Node.js, REST APIs, Git, and TypeScript. While my primary backend experience is with Express.js rather than NestJS, the architectural patterns — route structuring, middleware, controller logic, and database integration — carry over cleanly, and NestJS sits comfortably within my learning curve.

Two projects demonstrate this most clearly. **SportNest**, a full-stack sports facility booking platform, required me to engineer JWT-based HTTPOnly cookie sessions, implement Google OAuth via Better Auth, build dynamic MongoDB filtering with real-time slot pricing logic, and deploy a CORS-safe Express backend on Render — all while maintaining a responsive Next.js 15 App Router frontend. **BookNest** pushed further on data integrity: I built a borrow workflow that checks and decrements `available_quantity` atomically before confirming reservations, preventing over-borrowing at the database query level rather than just the UI level. These weren't tutorial builds — they involved real architectural decisions under actual constraints.

JANTRA's no-degree, skills-first philosophy resonates with how I've approached my development: every project was a deliberate attempt to solve a harder problem than the last. I want to join a team that evaluates code quality and problem-solving over credentials, and contribute to building reliable software that ships.

I'd welcome the opportunity to discuss how my work aligns with what your team is building.

Warm regards,
Kazi Md. Osman Goni

JobListing.title: Junior Web Developer
JobListing.description:
JANTRA is hiring a Remote Junior Web Developer to join our growing team. This role is ideal for someone who wants to build modern, scalable, and reliable web applications using technologies like React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL.

We value practical skills, problem-solving ability, clean coding habits, and a strong willingness to learn. Formal degrees are not our priority — your ability to build, improve, and grow matters most.

Salary: BDT 20,000 – 25,000
Work Type: Remote
Apply: https://jantrasoft.online/careers
responsibilities: - Build and maintain modern web applications using React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL.
- Convert UI/UX designs into responsive, clean, and user-friendly web pages.
- Develop and integrate REST APIs for web applications.
- Work with databases, write queries, and manage basic backend data flow.
- Fix bugs, improve performance, and maintain code quality.
- Collaborate with the team to understand project requirements and deliver tasks on time.
- Learn new tools, follow best practices, and continuously improve your development skills.
requirements: - Basic to intermediate knowledge of React, Next.js, Node.js, NestJS, and PostgreSQL.
- Understanding of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and TypeScript.
- Ability to build responsive and user-friendly web interfaces.
- Basic understanding of REST APIs and backend development.
- Ability to write clean, readable, and maintainable code.
- Good problem-solving skills and willingness to learn.
- Ability to work remotely, communicate clearly, and complete tasks on time.
- Degree is not required — skills, mindset, and consistency matter most.

Project.title: Horos Luxury Showroom
Project.description: A cinematic digital showroom for artisanal timepieces, where technical precision meets high-end industrial aesthetics. The experience transforms simple browsing into a curated gallery tour.
Project.challenge: The objective was to translate the tactile, micro-mechanical feel of physical watchmaking into a digital surface that conveys weight, depth, and prestige through fluid motion.
Project.approach: We executed a "Mechanical Mirror" strategy, focusing on high-contrast editorial photography, glassmorphic HUD telemetry, and 3D-inspired parallax rotations that respond to interaction.
Project.results: Established a definitive luxury benchmark for the brand, resulting in an average dwell time increase of 85% on project detail views.

Project.title: Brand Story Creative Landing Experience
Project.description: High-conversion brand website with a bold hero section, motion-led storytelling, and clear call-to-action structure optimized for service inquiries.
Project.challenge: The brand needed a premium digital presence that could immediately communicate creative quality and convert first-time visitors without visual clutter.
Project.approach: Built a clean grid-based interface with strong typography contrast, branded blue accents, video-first hero framing, and simplified navigation to reduce friction.
features: Minimal navigation with clear conversion path
Responsive layout for desktop and mobile
Project.results: Improved visual clarity, stronger brand perception, and faster path from first impression to inquiry action.

Project.title: Orange Commerce
Project.description: Orange Commerce is a high-fidelity e-commerce ecosystem designed for the modern luxury market. It prioritizes visual storytelling and fluid interaction patterns to transform standard shopping into a premium digital experience.
Project.challenge: The primary challenge was to bridge the gap between high-performance technical requirements and a boutique fashion aesthetic, ensuring that dense product information remains legible and evocative.
Project.approach: We implemented a "Studio" design philosophy, utilizing a 2-column mobile-first layout and heavy glassmorphism. This approach allows for a breathable yet information-dense surface that feels both technical and luxurious.
Project.results: Successfully launched with a 40% increase in user engagement compared to traditional templates, establishing a new benchmark for luxury e-commerce in the region.

Project.title: Prokash ADD
Project.description: Prokash ADD is a next-generation business management intelligence platform designed specifically for the Bangladeshi market. It reimagines the traditional ledger as a high-end digital conservatory.
Project.challenge: The challenge was to handle complex bilingual typographic hierarchies (Bangla and English) while maintaining a clean, industrial-luxury aesthetic that doesn't overwhelm small business users.
Project.approach: Our "Verdant Ledger" strategy utilized nature-inspired tonal layering and intentional asymmetry. We prioritized high-contrast typographic scales and replaced traditional 1px borders with white space for a premium editorial feel.
Project.results: Streamlined financial operations for over 100 early-adopter businesses, reducing manual entry time by average 60% through intuitive UX patterns.

Project.title: Evergreen International
Project.description: A premium digital sanctuary for high-end education, balancing technical precision with a nocturnal, glassmorphic atmosphere. "The Digital Conservatory" reimagines school portals as elite, curated environments.
Project.challenge: The objective was to move away from the clinical, utilitarian feel of standard educational platforms and create an immersive vault that feels machined from glass and light.
Project.approach: We embraced "Intentional Asymmetry" and "Tonal Depth," layering Space Grotesk headlines over translucent containers to create a technical yet luxurious editorial experience.

Project.title: Restaurant Reservation Website
Project.description: A fully immersive 3D restaurant reservation platform where users can explore a virtual restaurant environment and book seats interactively. The system provides a premium dining preview experience with real-time availability, smooth booking flow, and visually rich UI designed for engagement and conversion.
Project.challenge: Traditional reservation systems are static and lack user engagement. Users cannot visualize seating, ambience, or table placement before booking. Restaurants also struggle with dynamic seat management, peak-time allocation, and reducing booking conflicts or no-shows.
Project.approach: uild a WebGL-powered 3D restaurant interface using interactive scene mapping where each table is an object with real-time availability states. Integrate a robust backend for booking synchronization, seat locking, and time-slot management. Enhance UX with smooth animations, guided booking flow, and mobile-first responsiveness while maintaining real-time database sync.
features: Table reservation with date & time selection
Admin panel to manage menu and reservations
techStack:
Frontend:

Next.js
Three.js (3D rendering)
React Three Fiber
Tailwind CSS

Backend:

Node.js (NestJS or Express)
Prisma ORM
PostgreSQL

Real-time Layer:

WebSockets (Socket.io)

Authentication:

NextAuth / JWT
Project.results:
< 2s initial load time (optimized 3D assets)
60 FPS smooth rendering in supported devices
99.9% booking consistency accuracy
< 300ms reservation response time
40% increase in user engagement vs traditional UI
Reduced booking conflicts by 90% via real-time locking system

Project.title: SuperShop Pos System
Project.description: A modern high-speed POS ecosystem built for supershops and retail businesses with a clean, minimal, and beginner-friendly interface. Designed for ultra-fast billing, barcode-based inventory handling, smart hotkey payment actions, and seamless day-to-day retail operations without complex workflows.
Project.challenge: Traditional POS systems are overloaded with complicated UI/UX, slow billing flows, and difficult staff onboarding. Retail businesses need a lightweight and fast system where cashiers can generate invoices instantly, manage inventory efficiently, and operate the system with minimal training.
Project.approach: Build a performance-optimized POS platform focused on speed, simplicity, and operational efficiency. Implement keyboard shortcut-driven billing workflows, instant barcode scanning/generation, real-time inventory sync, and a responsive interface that allows staff to complete transactions in seconds.
Project.results:
3x faster checkout processing
70% reduced cashier operation time
Instant bill generation under 2 seconds
99.9% billing accuracy
Real-time inventory synchronization
Reduced employee training complexity by 60%
Optimized for high-volume supershop transactions

Project.title: POS Management System for Restaurants
Project.description: A high-performance, cloud-native Point of Sale ecosystem designed to unify retail operations through a scalable microservices architecture, providing real-time inventory synchronization and a seamless, lightning-fast checkout experience.
Project.challenge: Bridging the gap between fragmented sales data and manual inventory management. Current legacy systems suffer from slow data synchronization, frequent stock discrepancies, and rigid architectures that fail to scale with modern retail demands.
Project.approach: Deploying a modular microservices framework using NestJS and Next.js. We prioritize a "Gateway-first" approach to ensure secure, centralized communication between the Sales and Inventory services, backed by a reactive frontend for real-time operational visibility.
techStack:
API Gateway
Database: PostgreSQL with TypeORM
Communication: REST API & WebSockets
Project.results:
35% Faster transaction processing time
100% Real-time stock level accuracy
<200ms API response latency via Gateway
50% Reduction in manual inventory auditing

Project.title: Pharmacy Management System
Project.description: Jantra Pharmacy Management is a lightweight, multi-tenant pharmacy system built for low-end devices. It helps pharmacy owners handle daily sales, inventory, purchases, suppliers, expenses, barcode printing, and reports in one fast web app with real-time dashboard updates.
Project.challenge: Most small pharmacies still rely on manual records or heavy software that is slow on older devices. We needed a simple system that is fast, easy to use, and scalable so multiple pharmacy clients can use the same platform without data mixing.
Project.approach: Build a clean Next.js + NestJS architecture with tenant isolation at API and database level (pharmacy_id), optimize UI for mobile/low-spec hardware, and keep business flows straightforward (POS, stock, purchase, reporting) so staff can use it with minimal training.
features: Fast POS billing with invoice generation
Multi-tenant data isolation per pharmacy account
Project.results:
Dashboard transaction counters now aligned to daily scope (today-only)
Tenant-safe architecture ensures zero cross-pharmacy data exposure
Optimized lightweight UI for low-end devices and mobile screens
Reduced manual operation time through unified POS + inventory + reporting workflows
